title: Do not hardcode secrets or long-lived credentials
text: Hardcoded tokens, passwords, API keys, and similar credentials create rotation, exposure, and audit problems. Prefer runtime-provided secrets with narrowly scoped access.
summary: Secret-like literals should live in secret management or deployment configuration, not in source.
embedding_text:
rule_no: SEC.1
pack_id: shared_security
language_id: shared
context_id: 
dialect_id: 
section: SEC
title: Do not hardcode secrets or long-lived credentials
summary: Secret-like literals should live in secret management or deployment configuration, not in source.
keywords: not, secrets, credentials, sec.1, hardcode, long-lived, secret-like, literals, should, live, secret, management
category: security
reviewability: auto_review
trigger_patterns: hardcoded_secret
fix_guidance: Move the secret into environment-backed configuration or a secret manager and inject it at runtime.
text: Hardcoded tokens, passwords, API keys, and similar credentials create rotation, exposure, and audit problems. Prefer runtime-provided secrets with narrowly scoped access.
fix_guidance: Move the secret into environment-backed configuration or a secret manager and inject it at runtime.

title: Build shell commands from trusted primitives, not concatenated strings
text: Command construction through string concatenation is fragile and exposes injection risk. Prefer argument vectors, explicit allow-lists, or dedicated APIs.
summary: Shell execution paths should not interpolate untrusted fragments into a command string.
embedding_text:
rule_no: SEC.2
pack_id: shared_security
language_id: shared
context_id: 
dialect_id: 
section: SEC
title: Build shell commands from trusted primitives, not concatenated strings
summary: Shell execution paths should not interpolate untrusted fragments into a command string.
keywords: shell, not, command, sec.2, build, commands, trusted, primitives, concatenated, strings, execution, paths
category: security
reviewability: auto_review
trigger_patterns: shell_injection
fix_guidance: Use structured subprocess or exec APIs that separate the executable from validated arguments.
text: Command construction through string concatenation is fragile and exposes injection risk. Prefer argument vectors, explicit allow-lists, or dedicated APIs.
fix_guidance: Use structured subprocess or exec APIs that separate the executable from validated arguments.

title: Parameterize SQL instead of composing query text dynamically
text: SQL should be parameterized through the database driver's bind facilities or a higher-level abstraction. Dynamic text assembly should be reserved for carefully controlled structural clauses.
summary: Query text built from variables is difficult to reason about and easy to misuse.
embedding_text:
rule_no: SEC.3
pack_id: shared_security
language_id: shared
context_id: 
dialect_id: 
section: SEC
title: Parameterize SQL instead of composing query text dynamically
summary: Query text built from variables is difficult to reason about and easy to misuse.
keywords: text, sql, query, should, sec.3, parameterize, instead, composing, dynamically, built, variables, difficult
category: security
reviewability: auto_review
trigger_patterns: sql_injection, dynamic_sql
fix_guidance: Bind values separately from the SQL string and constrain structural query changes through allow-listed fragments.
text: SQL should be parameterized through the database driver's bind facilities or a higher-level abstraction. Dynamic text assembly should be reserved for carefully controlled structural clauses.
fix_guidance: Bind values separately from the SQL string and constrain structural query changes through allow-listed fragments.

title: Do not leave fallback secrets or test credentials in production code paths
text: Even when intended only for local development or bootstrap, embedded fallback secrets create a second and weaker trust path that is easy to forget during incident response or key rotation.
summary: Backup credentials often become the least-audited credential in the system.
embedding_text:
rule_no: SEC.4
pack_id: shared_security
language_id: shared
context_id: 
dialect_id: 
section: SEC
title: Do not leave fallback secrets or test credentials in production code paths
summary: Backup credentials often become the least-audited credential in the system.
keywords: fallback, secrets, credentials, sec.4, not, leave, test, production, code, paths, backup, often
category: security
reviewability: auto_review
trigger_patterns: hardcoded_secret
fix_guidance: Remove embedded fallback credentials and require runtime configuration or a dedicated local-only development path instead.
text: Even when intended only for local development or bootstrap, embedded fallback secrets create a second and weaker trust path that is easy to forget during incident response or key rotation.
fix_guidance: Remove embedded fallback credentials and require runtime configuration or a dedicated local-only development path instead.

title: Keep command execution APIs structured so the executable and arguments remain distinct
text: Separating the executable from validated arguments narrows the injection surface and makes the trust boundary visible in code review across languages and runtimes.
summary: Security review is much stronger when command construction never depends on ad hoc shell parsing.
embedding_text:
rule_no: SEC.5
pack_id: shared_security
language_id: shared
context_id: 
dialect_id: 
section: SEC
title: Keep command execution APIs structured so the executable and arguments remain distinct

keywords: command, executable, arguments, review, sec.5, keep, execution, apis, structured, remain, distinct, security
category: security
reviewability: auto_review
trigger_patterns: shell_injection
fix_guidance: Use structured process APIs, validate each argument according to its contract, and avoid passing shell fragments through string concatenation.
text: Separating the executable from validated arguments narrows the injection surface and makes the trust boundary visible in code review across languages and runtimes.
fix_guidance: Use structured process APIs, validate each argument according to its contract, and avoid passing shell fragments through string concatenation.

title: Dynamic query structure should stay allow-listed even when values are parameterized
text: ORDER BY fragments, table identifiers, and optional clauses can still create injection or correctness risk when the allowed structures are not tightly bounded.
summary: Parameter binding protects values, not uncontrolled structural SQL changes.

language_id: shared
context_id: 
dialect_id: 
section: SEC
title: Dynamic query structure should stay allow-listed even when values are parameterized

category: security
reviewability: auto_review
trigger_patterns: dynamic_sql
fix_guidance: Keep structural variation behind a small allow-list and continue binding values separately from the SQL text.
text: ORDER BY fragments, table identifiers, and optional clauses can still create injection or correctness risk when the allowed structures are not tightly bounded.
fix_guidance: Keep structural variation behind a small allow-list and continue binding values separately from the SQL text.

title: Example, bootstrap, and fallback credentials in committed code should be treated as real secrets unless clearly impossible to activate
text: Backup and bootstrap secrets often become the least-audited authentication path in a system because everyone assumes they are temporary or unreachable.
summary: "Test" or "dev" labels do not remove rotation, misuse, or confusion risk once credentials are committed.
embedding_text:
rule_no: SEC.7
pack_id: shared_security
language_id: shared
context_id: 
dialect_id: 
section: SEC
title: Example, bootstrap, and fallback credentials in committed code should be treated as real secrets unless clearly impossible to activate
summary: "Test" or "dev" labels do not remove rotation, misuse, or confusion risk once credentials are committed.
keywords: bootstrap, credentials, secrets, sec.7, example, fallback, committed, code, should, treated, real, unless
category: security
reviewability: auto_review
trigger_patterns: hardcoded_secret
fix_guidance: Remove committed fallback credentials or move them into an isolated local-only development path that cannot ship as a production default.
text: Backup and bootstrap secrets often become the least-audited authentication path in a system because everyone assumes they are temporary or unreachable.
fix_guidance: Remove committed fallback credentials or move them into an isolated local-only development path that cannot ship as a production default.

title: Avoid mutable latest tags for base images
text: A mutable base image tag means the same Dockerfile can build materially different outputs over time without a repository change.
summary: Floating base images reduce reproducibility and complicate incident rollback.
embedding_text:
rule_no: DOCKER.1
pack_id: dockerfile_best_practices
language_id: dockerfile
context_id: 
dialect_id: 
section: DOCKER
title: Avoid mutable latest tags for base images
summary: Floating base images reduce reproducibility and complicate incident rollback.
keywords: base, mutable, images, docker.1, avoid, latest, tags, floating, reduce, reproducibility, complicate, incident
category: configuration
reviewability: auto_review
trigger_patterns: latest_tag
fix_guidance: Pin the base image to an explicit version or digest appropriate for the release process.
text: A mutable base image tag means the same Dockerfile can build materially different outputs over time without a repository change.
fix_guidance: Pin the base image to an explicit version or digest appropriate for the release process.

title: Keep package index refresh and install in one layer
text: Package manager metadata refresh is safest when it stays coupled to the package install in the same build step.
summary: Splitting update and install steps invites stale cache behavior.
embedding_text:
rule_no: DOCKER.2
pack_id: dockerfile_best_practices
language_id: dockerfile
context_id: 
dialect_id: 
section: DOCKER
title: Keep package index refresh and install in one layer
summary: Splitting update and install steps invites stale cache behavior.
keywords: package, install, refresh, docker.2, keep, index, one, layer, splitting, update, steps, invites
category: configuration
reviewability: auto_review
trigger_patterns: apt_get_update_install_split
fix_guidance: Combine update and install in a single RUN layer and clean package caches deliberately where appropriate.
text: Package manager metadata refresh is safest when it stays coupled to the package install in the same build step.
fix_guidance: Combine update and install in a single RUN layer and clean package caches deliberately where appropriate.

title: Pin base images tightly enough that rebuilds remain reproducible across release windows
text: A semver-looking or distro-flavored base image tag can still be retargeted by the publisher. Digest pinning keeps the exact runtime input stable enough for rebuild explanation, rollback analysis, and supply-chain review.
summary: Version tags without immutable digests can still drift under the same source revision.
embedding_text:
rule_no: DOCKER.3
pack_id: dockerfile_best_practices
language_id: dockerfile
context_id: 
dialect_id: 
section: DOCKER
title: Pin base images tightly enough that rebuilds remain reproducible across release windows
summary: Version tags without immutable digests can still drift under the same source revision.
keywords: base, enough, can, still, docker.3, pin, images, tightly, rebuilds, remain, reproducible, across
category: configuration
reviewability: auto_review
trigger_patterns: latest_tag, base_tag_without_digest
fix_guidance: Prefer an immutable base reference such as `image:tag@sha256:...` when the build needs stable provenance and repeatable rebuild behavior.
text: A semver-looking or distro-flavored base image tag can still be retargeted by the publisher. Digest pinning keeps the exact runtime input stable enough for rebuild explanation, rollback analysis, and supply-chain review.
fix_guidance: Prefer an immutable base reference such as `image:tag@sha256:...` when the build needs stable provenance and repeatable rebuild behavior.

title: Keep broad context copies late and minimal so layer reuse and review stay predictable
text: Docker builds are easier to reason about when dependency installation, tool bootstrap, and application source copies are separated deliberately instead of importing the entire tree too early.
summary: Earlier broad COPY steps increase cache invalidation and make the image contract harder to inspect.
embedding_text:
rule_no: DOCKER.4
pack_id: dockerfile_best_practices
language_id: dockerfile
context_id: 
dialect_id: 
section: DOCKER
title: Keep broad context copies late and minimal so layer reuse and review stay predictable
summary: Earlier broad COPY steps increase cache invalidation and make the image contract harder to inspect.
keywords: broad, copies, docker.4, keep, context, late, minimal, layer, reuse, review, stay, predictable
category: container_hygiene
reviewability: auto_review
trigger_patterns: copy_dot
fix_guidance: Copy dependency manifests first, install dependencies, then copy only the application files required for the final stage.
text: Docker builds are easier to reason about when dependency installation, tool bootstrap, and application source copies are separated deliberately instead of importing the entire tree too early.
fix_guidance: Copy dependency manifests first, install dependencies, then copy only the application files required for the final stage.

title: Avoid remote URL ADD so external fetches remain explicit, pinned, and reviewable
text: Explicit fetch steps make version pinning, checksums, and provenance review easier than a remote ADD instruction that combines acquisition and layer creation.
summary: Dockerfile syntax should not hide the point where the build acquires an external artifact.
embedding_text:
rule_no: DOCKER.5
pack_id: dockerfile_best_practices
language_id: dockerfile
context_id: 
dialect_id: 
section: DOCKER
title: Avoid remote URL ADD so external fetches remain explicit, pinned, and reviewable
summary: Dockerfile syntax should not hide the point where the build acquires an external artifact.
keywords: remote, add, external, explicit, docker.5, avoid, url, fetches, remain, pinned, reviewable, dockerfile
category: configuration
reviewability: auto_review
trigger_patterns: add_remote_url
fix_guidance: Prefer a dedicated RUN fetch step with explicit versioning and verification, or copy in a vetted artifact from the build context.
text: Explicit fetch steps make version pinning, checksums, and provenance review easier than a remote ADD instruction that combines acquisition and layer creation.
fix_guidance: Prefer a dedicated RUN fetch step with explicit versioning and verification, or copy in a vetted artifact from the build context.

title: Full package upgrade inside image builds should be an intentional base-image ownership decision
text: When a Dockerfile runs a broad upgrade, reviewers should treat it as a supply and reproducibility choice rather than a routine install convenience.
summary: Unbounded distro upgrades reduce reproducibility and move responsibility away from the chosen base image contract.
embedding_text:
rule_no: DOCKER.6
pack_id: dockerfile_best_practices
language_id: dockerfile
context_id: 
dialect_id: 
section: DOCKER
title: Full package upgrade inside image builds should be an intentional base-image ownership decision
summary: Unbounded distro upgrades reduce reproducibility and move responsibility away from the chosen base image contract.
keywords: upgrade, image, should, reproducibility, docker.6, full, package, inside, builds, intentional, base-image, ownership
category: configuration
reviewability: auto_review
trigger_patterns: apt_upgrade
fix_guidance: Prefer a fresher pinned base image or a tightly scoped package install over full in-image upgrades when possible.
text: When a Dockerfile runs a broad upgrade, reviewers should treat it as a supply and reproducibility choice rather than a routine install convenience.
fix_guidance: Prefer a fresher pinned base image or a tightly scoped package install over full in-image upgrades when possible.

title: Multi-stage runtime stages should not inherit an entire /usr/local tree from the builder
text: Copying `/usr/local` wholesale from a builder stage into the runtime image can drag compilers, package-manager state, or bootstrap residue across the stage boundary even when the final image only needs one built artifact and the COPY line adds ownership-fixing flags or inline notes.
summary: Whole-prefix builder copies hide which toolchain and bootstrap artifacts reach the production image even when ownership-fixing COPY flags are present.
embedding_text:
rule_no: DOCKER.7
pack_id: dockerfile_best_practices
language_id: dockerfile
context_id: 
dialect_id: 
section: DOCKER
title: Multi-stage runtime stages should not inherit an entire /usr/local tree from the builder
summary: Whole-prefix builder copies hide which toolchain and bootstrap artifacts reach the production image even when ownership-fixing COPY flags are present.
keywords: builder, image, runtime, usr, local, bootstrap, even, when, ownership-fixing, copy, flags, stage
category: container_hygiene
reviewability: auto_review
trigger_patterns: copy_from_builder_usr_local
fix_guidance: Copy only the built binary, wheel, dist directory, or other minimal runtime artifact paths from the builder stage instead of mirroring `/usr/local` wholesale.
text: Copying `/usr/local` wholesale from a builder stage into the runtime image can drag compilers, package-manager state, or bootstrap residue across the stage boundary even when the final image only needs one built artifact and the COPY line adds ownership-fixing flags or inline notes.
fix_guidance: Copy only the built binary, wheel, dist directory, or other minimal runtime artifact paths from the builder stage instead of mirroring `/usr/local` wholesale.

title: Prefer non-root runtime identities
text: Running the application process as root increases the blast radius of an application compromise and makes defense-in-depth assumptions weaker.
summary: Containers should not run as root by default unless the workload truly requires it.
embedding_text:
rule_no: DOCKER.SEC.1
pack_id: container_security
language_id: dockerfile
context_id: 
dialect_id: 
section: DOCKERSEC
title: Prefer non-root runtime identities
summary: Containers should not run as root by default unless the workload truly requires it.
keywords: root, application, docker.sec.1, prefer, non-root, runtime, identities, containers, should, not, run, default
category: security
reviewability: auto_review
trigger_patterns: root_user
fix_guidance: Use a non-root USER and prepare filesystem ownership so the runtime does not depend on root privileges.
text: Running the application process as root increases the blast radius of an application compromise and makes defense-in-depth assumptions weaker.
fix_guidance: Use a non-root USER and prepare filesystem ownership so the runtime does not depend on root privileges.

title: Keep COPY scopes narrow enough to exclude accidental secrets and cache churn
text: Broad COPY patterns reduce cache effectiveness and increase the chance that temporary files or unreviewed assets leak into the image.
summary: Copying the entire build context makes secrets inclusion and noisy rebuilds more likely.
embedding_text:
rule_no: DOCKER.SEC.2
pack_id: container_security
language_id: dockerfile
context_id: 
dialect_id: 
section: DOCKERSEC
title: Keep COPY scopes narrow enough to exclude accidental secrets and cache churn
summary: Copying the entire build context makes secrets inclusion and noisy rebuilds more likely.
keywords: copy, secrets, cache, docker.sec.2, keep, scopes, narrow, enough, exclude, accidental, churn, copying
category: security
reviewability: auto_review
trigger_patterns: copy_dot
fix_guidance: Copy only the files needed for the build stage and keep ignored files out of the build context.
text: Broad COPY patterns reduce cache effectiveness and increase the chance that temporary files or unreviewed assets leak into the image.
fix_guidance: Copy only the files needed for the build stage and keep ignored files out of the build context.

title: Limit root to build-time setup and avoid carrying it into the final runtime image
text: A Dockerfile may legitimately need root during package installation or ownership fixes, but the release runtime should still step down to a narrowly scoped user before the application starts.
summary: The final application container should not inherit broader privilege than the workload needs.
embedding_text:
rule_no: DOCKER.SEC.3
pack_id: container_security
language_id: dockerfile
context_id: 
dialect_id: 
section: DOCKERSEC
title: Limit root to build-time setup and avoid carrying it into the final runtime image
summary: The final application container should not inherit broader privilege than the workload needs.
keywords: root, final, runtime, application, should, docker.sec.3, limit, build-time, setup, avoid, carrying, image
category: security
reviewability: auto_review
trigger_patterns: root_user
fix_guidance: Finish privileged setup in build layers and switch the final stage or runtime command to a non-root user.
text: A Dockerfile may legitimately need root during package installation or ownership fixes, but the release runtime should still step down to a narrowly scoped user before the application starts.
fix_guidance: Finish privileged setup in build layers and switch the final stage or runtime command to a non-root user.

title: Broad COPY patterns should be paired with strict context hygiene or replaced with targeted copies
text: A full-context COPY is easy to write but difficult to audit over time. New local files, credentials, and build artifacts can slip into the image unless the context boundary remains tightly controlled.
summary: Copying the full build context widens the boundary for secrets, tooling residue, and accidental runtime inputs.
embedding_text:
rule_no: DOCKER.SEC.4
pack_id: container_security
language_id: dockerfile
context_id: 
dialect_id: 
section: DOCKERSEC
title: Broad COPY patterns should be paired with strict context hygiene or replaced with targeted copies
summary: Copying the full build context widens the boundary for secrets, tooling residue, and accidental runtime inputs.
keywords: context, copy, build, boundary, docker.sec.4, broad, patterns, should, paired, strict, hygiene, replaced
category: security
reviewability: auto_review
trigger_patterns: copy_dot
fix_guidance: Narrow the COPY set or maintain a strict .dockerignore policy that excludes secrets, caches, and local tooling output.
text: A full-context COPY is easy to write but difficult to audit over time. New local files, credentials, and build artifacts can slip into the image unless the context boundary remains tightly controlled.
fix_guidance: Narrow the COPY set or maintain a strict .dockerignore policy that excludes secrets, caches, and local tooling output.

title: Do not pipe remote downloads directly into a shell during image build
text: A Docker build that executes remote content directly removes the review point where fetched artifacts can be pinned, inspected, or verified.
summary: Build-time bootstrap commands deserve the same provenance boundary as checked-in install scripts.
embedding_text:
rule_no: DOCKER.SEC.5
pack_id: container_security
language_id: dockerfile
context_id: 
dialect_id: 
section: DOCKERSEC
title: Do not pipe remote downloads directly into a shell during image build
summary: Build-time bootstrap commands deserve the same provenance boundary as checked-in install scripts.
keywords: remote, directly, build, docker.sec.5, not, pipe, downloads, shell, during, image, build-time, bootstrap
category: security
reviewability: auto_review
trigger_patterns: curl_pipe_shell_run
fix_guidance: Fetch artifacts explicitly, pin versions, verify integrity or signatures, and execute them as a separate audited build step.
text: A Docker build that executes remote content directly removes the review point where fetched artifacts can be pinned, inspected, or verified.
fix_guidance: Fetch artifacts explicitly, pin versions, verify integrity or signatures, and execute them as a separate audited build step.

title: Remote URL ADD expands trust to an external fetch boundary that should stay explicit
text: Using ADD with a remote URL pulls an external artifact during build without the explicit versioning and verification structure reviewers usually expect around fetch steps.
summary: Hidden network fetches in Dockerfile syntax make provenance and cache behavior harder to review.
embedding_text:
rule_no: DOCKER.SEC.6
pack_id: container_security
language_id: dockerfile
context_id: 
dialect_id: 
section: DOCKERSEC
title: Remote URL ADD expands trust to an external fetch boundary that should stay explicit
summary: Hidden network fetches in Dockerfile syntax make provenance and cache behavior harder to review.
keywords: remote, url, add, external, fetch, explicit, docker.sec.6, expands, trust, boundary, should, stay
category: security
reviewability: auto_review
trigger_patterns: add_remote_url
fix_guidance: Replace remote URL ADD with an explicit fetch-plus-verify step or vendor the artifact through a more reviewable build input path.
text: Using ADD with a remote URL pulls an external artifact during build without the explicit versioning and verification structure reviewers usually expect around fetch steps.
fix_guidance: Replace remote URL ADD with an explicit fetch-plus-verify step or vendor the artifact through a more reviewable build input path.

title: Build-time credentials should not ride through ARG or ENV when BuildKit secret mounts are available
text: Docker build credentials should stay out of the normal Dockerfile variable surface, including authenticated URLs assigned through ARG or ENV, when a secret mount or another external secret injection path can provide them only to the step that needs them.
summary: Credential-bearing ARG and ENV values, including inline authenticated URLs, are easier to leak through image history, layer metadata, and downstream reuse.
embedding_text:
rule_no: DOCKER.SEC.7
pack_id: container_security
language_id: dockerfile
context_id: 
dialect_id: 
section: DOCKERSEC
title: Build-time credentials should not ride through ARG or ENV when BuildKit secret mounts are available
summary: Credential-bearing ARG and ENV values, including inline authenticated URLs, are easier to leak through image history, layer metadata, and downstream reuse.
keywords: through, arg, env, secret, credentials, should, when, including, authenticated, urls, docker.sec.7, build-time
category: security
reviewability: auto_review
trigger_patterns: build_secret_arg_env, build_secret_arg_env_authenticated_url
fix_guidance: Pass the credential through a BuildKit `RUN --mount=type=secret` step or another external secret injection path instead of baking it into ARG or ENV.
text: Docker build credentials should stay out of the normal Dockerfile variable surface, including authenticated URLs assigned through ARG or ENV, when a secret mount or another external secret injection path can provide them only to the step that needs them.
fix_guidance: Pass the credential through a BuildKit `RUN --mount=type=secret` step or another external secret injection path instead of baking it into ARG or ENV.
